name: good-arxiv

env_axis.name: shift-axis
env_axis.shift_mechanism: arXiv papers + citation graph with covariate (year/domain/word) and concept (year/domain/word) shifts as parallel axes

facts: arXiv papers + citation graph; covariate splits (year/domain/word) and concept splits (year/domain/word) on the same underlying data.
hypotheses: Within GOOD-Arxiv, covariate-year and concept-year splits at the same year boundary expose different methods, providing a rare clean test of GOOD's theoretical dichotomy.
GOOD-Arxiv's text+graph hybrid behaves intermediate between pure-text and pure-graph rather than categorically distinct from both.
intuitions: A three-way cross-comparison of GOOD-Arxiv vs ogbn-arxiv vs Wild-Time-arXiv is feasible — three different splits on similar underlying data that would directly expose split-as-confounder.
Foundation-model results from 2024-25 on GOOD-Arxiv specifically likely exist.
unknowns: Whether the empirical separation of covariate-year vs concept-year tracks the theoretical intent cannot be determined — GOOD operationalizes the distinction via specific split rules, so the paper's distinction is operational, not theoretical.
epistemic_state.boundary: Inherits the C=0 of the arXiv data; small-scale per split.

representational_commitments: Covariate vs concept operationalized via specific split rules (not theory)
3 domain choices × 2 shift types = 6 splits curated
Text and graph treated as one domain (typed Ty=3)
